title: Hide Weekends in Calendar View (Boards and Dashboards)
excerpt: My company works a standard Mon-Fri work week. We need the ability to toggle the weekend days in the calendar view on/off so we don’t have so much wasted space when viewing/displaying the calendar view for our Boards AND…

posts.author: cdf (Carrie d.)
posts.cookedHtml: My company works a standard Mon-Fri work week. We need the ability to toggle the weekend days in the calendar view on/off so we don’t have so much wasted space when viewing/displaying the calendar view for our Boards AND Dashboards. Per my conversation with support, this is only available for Timeline view.

posts.author: sybr (adam)
posts.cookedHtml: Receiving multiple requests from end users for this feature.

posts.author: jpv (Jennifer)
posts.cookedHtml: This is key - so many organizations using monday.com don’t need this wasted space of weekends. Seems like it should be simple.

posts.author: s.oliveiralui (Lui Oliveira)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was also looking for that option… But I don’t agree to hide the weekends as default. For example, I work for a company in Israel, they don’t work on Fridays but they work on Sundays. I work from Monday to Friday because I’m not there.

But it’s definitely missing an option to block weekends, or block days on each account. 'Cause depending on where you work, work days can be different.

posts.author: 33Harlow (Kris Harlow)
posts.cookedHtml: Just chiming in, because this would be awesome! The calendar view is our go-to for high-level review of projects, and having the Sunday and Saturday on screen really just clutters the view, and occasionally causes some confusion.

posts.author: Always2Frustrated (Ryan Cooper)
posts.cookedHtml: Ditto! I have the same concern too!

posts.author: freek-gcompany (Freek van de Griendt)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here, got the request from different customers.

posts.author: AngieAngeles (Angie Angeles )
posts.cookedHtml: Would love this feature. My workaround is to export to Google Calendar, and choose not to show weekends there, but lose other functionality of Monday in doing so.

posts.author: mnewland (Maddy Newland)
posts.cookedHtml: 100% need this! It’s just a checkbox for the gannt chart view so please can we have the option to hide/view weekends on the calendar view. It’s causing a lot of confusion with the team and client.

posts.author: Zoe (Zoe)
posts.cookedHtml: Seems crazy this option doesn’t exist.

posts.author: lbrown (Linnea)
posts.cookedHtml: I was just recently looking for this option as well! M-F work schedule seems common for a lot of people so I agree that it’s annoying that it’s not an option to hide weekends when you’re looking at the calendar view. You can ‘hide’ weekends from the timeline so I was thinking that’d work in conjunction to the calendar. I’m a visual person so I think it makes managing projects slightly irritating when using the calendar view.

posts.author: bflory (Brett Flory)
posts.cookedHtml: Either do this or allow me to make Saturday & Sunday much smaller. Thanks.

posts.author: iamvenumous (Chris Hollingsworth)
posts.cookedHtml: No response from Dev’s after 5 months and 20+ up votes? I’m starting to feel like these boards just aren’t being taken seriously.

posts.author: BoyanIliev (Boyan Iliev)
posts.cookedHtml: Joining in on this feature request.

posts.author: Sjurd (Sjurd Skutlaberg)
posts.cookedHtml: Joining in on this request too!

posts.author: LyytiMaria (Maria Saaristo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yup, much needed here too! Especially since the padding is quite wide on the items when viewed in a calendar, the calendar needs to be unpractically wide for seeing the items’ names. This combined with the unnecessary weekends makes the calendar hard to read.

Also, I’d love if the cells would adjust to the number of items on a day. For a busy day the “week row” would show all items instead of “+3” without making all week rows as tall

posts.author: SilviaSa (Silvia Assumpção Soares de Sá)
posts.cookedHtml: Would love this feature too!

posts.author: Gbolyard (Garrick Bolyard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear Monday,
Deal with this ASAP. How was this not done out of the gate, what a loss. The visual real estate it takes up is insane. This is a five minute fix. Could not be more of a low hanging fruit.

posts.author: Gbolyard (Garrick Bolyard)
posts.cookedHtml:
An update to my plight. Got an email from the Ceo response team.
They said sorry, they are working on it, and can I use the Gantt chart instead. The calendar view is for my clients, who are not tech people. I want them to have a way to understand the project in a well known format.

Since this issue has been asked for since 2020, I have no confidence this is in the pipeline to get fixed.

This is really lame on Mondays part. I cant imagine an easier fix. Really disappointing.

posts.author: tjb (Troy Brophy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Why does monday.com have an admin setting that allows you to leave weekends out of timelines, and then show you weekends on the calendars that pull data from the timelines?

Like many things with this app, it makes no Earthly sense.

posts.author: Gbolyard (Garrick Bolyard)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am with you. I found another problem with the calendar, that I have put in for feature iteration. The calendar view seems to be the least iterated version of data management. I am exceptionally displeased with the calendar view.

Such basic and low hanging fruit, and yet the Monday dev’s are spending time and resources on niche things that few will benefit from.

posts.author: Gbolyard (Garrick Bolyard)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday Still has not even attempted to deal with this. Really sad. I am not sure what else to do other than bug Monday. This is a easy fix, obviously lots of people want it.

posts.author: meena.toor (Meena Toor)
posts.cookedHtml: This so needed. So simple too!!! Let’s do it Monday team!

posts.author: Eunice (Eunice)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any update on when this feature is likely to be added - causing issues for us now as well.

posts.author: Morgan_Hope (Morgan LeBaige)
posts.cookedHtml: Still no updates on this from Monday support?

posts.author: ApeyDave (Dave)
posts.cookedHtml:
This appears to be an option now.
Within the calendar view, click Settings, and “Hide Weekends” is in the Additional settings category.

posts.author: neilhunt (Neil Hunt)
posts.cookedHtml: We have a number of teams, with their own workspaces, working in monday. Some teams wish to hide weekends (to avoid scheduling work over the weekends) but some teams need to show weekends (for shows/events they are organising). At the moment, you can only show/hide weekends at Account Level. Can you introduce a new feature so that it can be toggled on/off by Workspace (or Board).

posts.author: Nayab_Abbasi (Nayab Abbasi)
posts.cookedHtml: Is it possible to have the option to show/hide weekends on a more granular level, such as workspace or board? The current system-wide setting causes problems when some users need weekends displayed for their project while others do not.

posts.author: nh27 (Nayan Hirani)
posts.cookedHtml: I second this, it would be a great help!

posts.author: zsbarnard (Zach Barnard (Altair Global))
posts.cookedHtml: I definitely second this request! This feature, at the board or workspace level, would be a game changer for my organization. Thanks for requesting this, Neil!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone! I am excited to share that after hearing your feedback and understanding the importance of this feature, our development team has been hard at work to bring this feature your boards!

The ability to hide weekends is currently in Alpha and being tested. It should be released to all users soon! I will update when I know more

If you want to be the first to know about updates to this feature, makes sure to turn on your notifications for this thread. How to turn on notifications for a topics

cc @amber

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! I’m very excited to share that the ability to hide weekends in Calendar View for both Boards and Dashboards is now fully released!

We heard your feedback about wanting to make the most of your calendar space and focus on what matters most during your work week, and we’re so glad to make this feature available to all users.

Thank you for consistently helping us shape the platform!
